steps.title: Identify the structure
steps.content: The blank contains "insecticide" followed by "sulfoxaflor." The word "sulfoxaflor" is an appositive identifying which specific insecticide was added. Rule: a restrictive appositive (identifies which specific thing) takes no commas. A non-restrictive appositive (adds optional extra info) takes commas on both sides.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
A. insecticide sulfoxaflor — no commas. Restrictive treatment. Reconstruct: "...added the common insecticide sulfoxaflor to a sucrose solution..." — "sulfoxaflor" specifies which insecticide; it is essential. Grammatically correct. Correct.

B. insecticide, sulfoxaflor, — commas on both sides treat "sulfoxaflor" as non-restrictive (removable). Reconstruct: "...added the common insecticide to a sucrose solution" — without the name, the sentence doesn't identify which insecticide. The name is essential, not optional. Eliminated.

C. insecticide sulfoxaflor, — comma after but not before creates an asymmetric, unpaired insertion. Reconstruct: "...added the common insecticide sulfoxaflor, to a sucrose solution..." — the stray comma incorrectly separates the object from its prepositional phrase. Eliminated.

D. insecticide, sulfoxaflor — comma before but not after. Reconstruct: "...added the common insecticide, sulfoxaflor to a sucrose solution..." — missing the closing comma makes this structurally broken. Eliminated.
